channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: U.S. raises alarm over Russian interference in Romania’s election
item.description: The Biden administration and lawmakers in Congress are raising alarm over Russian interference in presidential elections in Romania, a NATO ally that borders Ukraine and the Black Sea.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Bangladesh court bans publication of speeches by ousted Prime Minister Hasina
item.description: A special tribunal in Bangladesh has banned the publication of any speeches by former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, who is in exile in India after being ousted in August after mass protests.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas official says Gaza ceasefire talks have resumed after weekslong hiatus
item.description: A Hamas official says that after a weekslong hiatus international mediators have resumed negotiating with the militant group and Israel over a ceasefire in Gaza, and that he was hopeful a deal to end the 14-month war was within reach.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba restores power grid and resumes planned blackouts of about 5 hours a day
item.description: After a nationwide blackout left millions without electricity for several hours in Cuba, the power grid has been restored, authorities said, adding that scheduled power outages will now resume.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodríguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Masked man arrested outside Swedish royal family residence with knife, handcuffs, tape: reports
item.description: A man acting suspiciously while armed outside the home of a Swedish royal family was arrested Wednesday evening, authorities told media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany hands over Australian ancestral remains held by museums for over 100 years
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Five sets of ancestral remains from Australia that had been in German museum collections since the 19th century were handed back at a ceremony Thursday that a community representative described as a sad but “very joyful” moment.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amnesty: Israel committing genocide in Gaza
item.description: Human rights group Amnesty International accused Israel of committing genocide in Gaza amid its ongoing war against Hamas in the region. In a report released Wednesday, the organization listed facts about the war, which began Oct.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UK leader Starmer vows to take on 'alliance of naysayers' as he outlines plan for change
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed Thursday to take on the “alliance of naysayers” and the bureaucratic “nonsense” that have hampered construction projects in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken, Russia's Lavrov clash on Ukraine at a security meeting in Malta
item.description: VALLETTA, Malta (AP) — Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov clashed Thursday indirectly with U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken at an annual security meeting, accusing the West of risking escalation over Ukraine but walking out before Blinken and other speakers could respond.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world’s tallest snowman, measuring 122 feet, built by residents in Bethel, Maine
item.description: In 2008, the residents of Bethel, Maine, spent a month creating what is still to this day the tallest snowwoman that has ever been made, according to Guinness World Records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US first lady Jill Biden in the capital of the United Arab Emirates on her last solo foreign trip
item.description: Jill Biden has used a tour of the United Arab Emirates’ capital to praise efforts to fight cancer and call for more attention on women’s health issues as part of her final solo foreign tour as first lady.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrian insurgents capture central city of Hama in severe setback to the Syrian president
item.description: Syrian insurgents have swept into the central city of Hama after days of intense clashes with government forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Opposition party leader arrested as Georgia authorities try to curb protests over ending EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgian police on Wednesday raided the offices of an opposition party and arrested its leader in an apparent attempt to squelch a wave of mass protests triggered by the governing party’s decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 hours of anger, bravery and defiance during martial law in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol shocked the nation when he declared martial law and called in troops to secure the National Assembly building where parliament sits.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French government collapses in no-confidence vote
item.description: French lawmakers ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier with a no-confidence vote in the government Wednesday, the first time since 1962.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers joined together Wednesday in a historic no-confidence vote prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his Cabinet members to resign, a first since 1962. The National Assembly approved the motion by 331 votes.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers hold moment of silence for slain Omer Neutra as thousands mourn in hometown synagogue
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers held a moment of silence for the slain Omer Neutra, an American-Israeli who was killed by Hamas on Oct. 7, 2023, according to new intelligence uncovered by the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers joined together to vote Wednesday a no-confidence motion prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier to resign. The National Assembly approved the motion by 331 votes. A minimum of 288 were needed.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Syria's insurgency leader tours seized city of Aleppo, as fierce battles intensify near Hama
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The leader of Syria's most powerful insurgent group toured the seized city of Aleppo on Wednesday in a surprise visit — the first since the group captured large parts of the city over the weekend as fierce fighting intensifies in the government-led counter-offensive in northern Hama.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's FBI pick Kash Patel targeted in Iranian cyberattack
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was among those targeted by an Iranian cyber attack. It’s unclear whether any information was gained through the attack, but it makes Patel the latest figure in the Trump campaign to be pursued by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: EU condemns reported Taliban move to suspend medical education for women and girls
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday condemned the Taliban for violating human rights and women's access to education after media reports that the Taliban's leader has ordered private and public institutions to stop providing medical courses for women and girls in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Archaeologists in Denmark discover over 100 weapons from the Iron Age during excavations
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark unearthed what they've suggested is a sacrifice of weapons. The weapons likely belonged to a powerful chieftain thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean president declares martial law in move against opposition party
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law on Tuesday and accused the opposition party of taking the parliamentary process hostage with "anti-state" activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation
